rulings.Case Title: GERARD MCMENAMY VS. DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Petition For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 2, 2013, Line 18, Amended Petition For Writ Of Mandate. The petition for writ of mandate is denied. The exclusionary rule does not apply to the DMV proceeding under the facts of this case. See Park v. Valverde (2007) 152 Cal. App. 4th 877. The officer had reasonable suspicion to stop petitioner's vehicle when he stopped six feet before the crosswalk, drove through the stop sign intersection at about 25 mph without stopping, continued driving although the officer had turned on his car red lights and siren, and then drove inbound into the outbound traffic lane before ultimately stopping in the road. Petitioner's premature stop 6 feet before the crosswalk was sufficient to warrant the officer's investigative stop. The officer's subsequent investigation, including the objective signs of intoxication, resulted in probable cause to believe petitioner was driving under the influence of alcohol. Petitioner was lawfully arrested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: REMY COINTREAU USA, INC A NEW YORK CORPORATION VS. BRIDGEVIEW ENTERPRISES, LLC D/B/A WINEGAVEL, A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 2, 2013, Line 10, PLAINTIFF REMY COINTREAU USA, INC.'s Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied without prejudice to filing a motion for summary judgment. Although the Court may take judicial notice of the existence of the August 9, 2012 Order Granting Motion Requesting Admissions Be Admitted, the Court does not take judicial notice of the truth of those statements for purposes of a motion for judgment on the pleadings. The same is not true for a motion for summary judgment. rulings.Case Title: CHRISTIAN ROETTGERS VS. AUTONET MOBILE, INC. A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions For Autonet'S Failure To Appear At Three Party Witness Depositons; And Imporper Instructions At The Continued Deposittions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 2, 2013, Line 4, PLAINTIFF CHRISTIAN ROETTGERS' Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions For Autonet's Failure To Appear At Three Party Witness Depositons; And Improper Instructions At The Continued Depositions Pro Tem Judge Arlene Barton, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion for Sanctions for Failure to Appear at Three Party Witnesses Depositions and For Improper Instructions at the Continued Deposition is Denied. The record clearly reflects plaintiff had agreed to move the deposition dates of the three party witnesses upon which Defendants relied. Plaintiff failed to provide evidence that the deposition of Ms. Bonetti was left open as to any and all questions; the transcripts fails to set forth clearly the full reason for a further deposition but it is clear there were certain objections that were made the basis for the further deposition. Further plaintiff did not provide a CRC 3.1345(a) statement as required. Sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to abarton@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ALAN OVSON VS. PETER KHOURY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Ofmotion And Omtion To Strike Attorneys Fees And Punitive And Exemplary Damages Adn Certain Other Allegations
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 2, 2013, Line 15, DEFENDANTS PETER KHOURY and H324 LLC's Motion To Strike Attorneys Fees And Punitive And Exemplary Damages And Certain Other Allegations. The motion to strike the request for attorney fees is denied. The only basis for fees that Plaintiff cites in opposition to the motion to strike is Civil Procedure Code section 1021.5. However, Plaintiff is not even required to plead his intent to seek attorneys' fees under CCP 1021.5. Snatchko v. Westfield LLC (2010) 187 Cal. App. 4th 469, 497. The Court thus denies the motion to strike this requested relief at the pleading stage. Finally, Defendant states in Reply that, despite the reference in the caption of his motion to strike to "punitive and exemplary damages," he was not moving to strike references to these damages. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: TRIMARK ERF, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. 2000 PLUS RESTAURANT EQUIPMENT INC.,A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 2, 2013, Line 15, PLAINTIFF TRIMARK ERF, INC.'s Application For Right To Attach Order And For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment. Denied without prejudice. Trimark ERF has not demonstrated the probable validity of its claim. The purported evidence attached to Mr. Kay's declaration as Exhibit A is not a statement "containing a running and current list of all of the invoices and or charges, and each of the same, which are presently due, setting forth the date of the invoice and/or charge, the due date thereof, the invoice and/or charge number and the amount thereof, indicating a present unpaid balance in the amount of $ 12,369.20 as of March 8, 2013" or a copy of "Plaintiff's journal page, ledger card and computer printout or otherwise itemized statement indicating all charges and payments applied to those invoice and/or charges." (Kay Decl., p. 2:15-19, 2:21-23.) Instead of attaching the described statement, Mr. Kay attaches copies of 3 invoices. (Kay Decl., Exhibit A.) Further, the 3 invoices attached as Exhibit A do not contain any provisions to demonstrate that 2000 Plus "agreed to such interest, service and finance charges, and each of them." (Id. at p. 4:6-8.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
